reasoning: The document contains 7 major defects across LLM fidelity and programmatic signals. These include flattened list hierarchies, visual duplication of text and images, displaced hyperlinks, retained headers/footers, and incomplete alt text. Because the total number of major defects exceeds the threshold of 5, the document must be rejected.

concerns.analysis: On page 11, the nested list under 'B. Information to be Provided in Blasting Plans' is structurally corrupted. In the source PDF, item '1.' is the parent to sub-items 'a.' through 'j.', and item '2.' ('If the maximum explosive weight...') is a sibling to item '1.'. In the JSON output, sub-items 'a.' through 'j.' are incorrectly placed at the same hierarchical level as item '1.' inside the 'children' array, flattening the list hierarchy. Furthermore, item '2.' is completely disconnected from this
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
B. Information to be Provided in Blasting Plans.
1. The information provided by Alamance Aggregates...
   a. Date and time...
   ... 
2. If the maximum explosive weight per delay will vary...

concerns.analysis: The HTML output contains instances where text content from the PDF is rendered both as structured HTML text and as embedded image screenshots. For example, on page 5 of the PDF, the text for numbered paragraphs 4 and 5 is extracted as HTML text, but an image with alt text describing those exact paragraphs ('The concluding section of a formal document featuring numbered paragraphs titled "4. Web-based Data"') is inserted immediately below them. A similar duplication occurs with an email from Rep.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: PDF Page 5 numbered paragraphs 4 and 5; PDF Page 4 email from Rep. Dennis Riddell.
concerns.output_quote:
• Web-based Data Room...
  • Permit Conditions Requested by Colonial...
concerns.location: HTML output corresponding to PDF pages 4 and 5

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (8 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=8. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 28 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=28, examples=['mailto:report.spam@nc.gov', 'http://www.colpipe.com', 'http://www.colpipe.com']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (17): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=17, pages_affected=16/17. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (1 instance(s)): p6→p7: list item ends “…rgency Access field roadway. To ensure a”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=1. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 12 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (12 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "... featuring the state seal and listing Governor Roy"; p1: "...ormation to complete an application process. Below"; p2: "...ermit application deadline. It features a sign-off"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=12 (truncated=12, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source document uses underline extensively for structural emphasis. For instance, on page 1, "Certified Mail" and "Return Receipt Requested" are underlined. In Attachment 1 (pages 11-14), the beginning of every list item (e.g., "A. Notice of Blasting Related Activities.", "B. Information to be Provided in Blasting Plans.") is underlined. Since the extraction JSON uses markdown which lacks an underline representation, this visual emphasis is completely lost in the output.

action_items: Correct list structural hierarchies and repair lists split across page breaks
Remove redundant full-page and screenshot images that duplicate extracted text
Ensure all hyperlinks are properly inline within paragraphs
Remove extraneous header and footer text from the structural output
Provide complete and meaningful alt text for all extracted images
